description: Refactor platform chart modules to read configuration from Pulumi stack config files instead of branching on the isLocal boolean, then add the dev stack configuration. This enables multi-environment support where adding a new environment requires only a new YAML file with zero code changes.

userStories.title: Refactor platform charts to use Pulumi stack config
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want the platform chart modules to read per-environment configuration from Pulumi stack config files instead of using the isLocal boolean, so that adding new environments requires only a new YAML file with zero code changes.

In monorepo/infra/platform/charts/cert-manager.ts:
- Replace isLocal ternaries with a resourceTier config key (minimal | standard)
- Define two inline resource presets; select based on config value
- Default to minimal (preserves current local behavior)

In monorepo/infra/platform/charts/monitoring.ts:
- Replace isLocal ternaries with config reads for resourceTier, prometheusRetention, prometheusScrapeInterval, prometheusStorageSize
- Grafana password: use config.get('grafanaAdminPassword') with fallback to 'admin' when unset (local), requireSecret() when set (dev/production)
- Defaults match current local values exactly

Update Pulumi.local.yaml and Pulumi.production.yaml with the new config keys so both existing environments produce zero diff on pulumi preview.
acceptanceCriteria: cert-manager.ts uses resourceTier config key instead of isLocal boolean
monitoring.ts reads prometheusRetention, prometheusScrapeInterval, prometheusStorageSize, and resourceTier from Pulumi config
Grafana password uses config.get() with fallback to 'admin' when unset
Pulumi.local.yaml has new config keys added
Pulumi.production.yaml has new config keys added
No references to isLocal remain in chart modules
Default values match current local behavior exactly
userStories.notes: Effort: M. This is the foundation task — must complete before dev stack config (TDP-004-03) can be added.

userStories.title: Add platform Pulumi dev stack configuration
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want a Pulumi.dev.yaml stack configuration for the platform project so that I can deploy the platform services to the dev cluster.

Create monorepo/infra/platform/Pulumi.dev.yaml with:
- Stack reference: chiply-org/tdp-cluster/dev
- Environment: dev, resource tier: standard
- Prometheus: 7d retention, 15s scrape, 10Gi storage
- Grafana admin password as Pulumi secret placeholder
acceptanceCriteria: grafanaAdminPassword is configured as a secure value placeholder
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Depends on US-001 (TDP-004-01) — config keys must exist in chart code first.
